title: 158 Combat Engagements, 9,414 Drones Launched; Russia Presses Spring Offensive
detail: Ukraine recorded 158 combat engagements on March 26, 2026 (Day 1,492). Russia conducted 70 airstrikes dropping 231 guided aerial bombs and launched 9,414 kamikaze drones — one of the highest daily drone totals of the war — with 4,184 artillery and MLRS attacks. Russian losses: 1,210 personnel killed or wounded; Ukrainian MoD cumulative total reached ~1,292,170 personnel since Feb 24, 2022. Ukrainian forces repelled 44 assault operations near Bilytske, Rodynske, Myrnohrad, Pokrovsk, Kotlyne, Udachne, and Novomykolaivka. Ukrainian advances were recorded near Oleksandrivka (western Zaporizhzhia Oblast) and in northern Kharkiv Oblast. Russian forces continued pressure in the Slovyansk direction, with Russian advances also noted near Pokrovsk and in the Kupyansk direction. Kharkiv casualties from March 25 strikes rose to 9.

media.caption: Ukraine fends off increased Russian attacks while striking oil infrastructure

title: Mandatory Evacuations Ordered in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk Districts as Russian Forces Advance
detail: Local authorities in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk — the northern anchor of Ukraine's 'Fortress Belt' defensive chain in Donetsk Oblast — ordered mandatory evacuations for families with children in districts closest to the fighting on March 26. Authorities removed trolleybuses, dismantled overhead wiring, and installed anti-drone nets in preparation for intensifying Russian pressure. The Fortress Belt stretches 50 km from Sloviansk through Kramatorsk to Druzhkivka and Kostiantynivka. ISW assessed Russia is unlikely to seize the Belt in 2026 but is pressing hard. Sustained attacks were reported across Donbas, Sumy, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Dnipropetrovsk, Kherson, Chernihiv, Mykolaiv, and Odesa regions over the preceding 24 hours.

media.caption: Mandatory evacuations ordered in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk as Russia's spring offensive advances

title: Ukraine Destroys Vsesvit-Oil Depot Near Dnipropetrovsk; Oil Tanker Seized in Mediterranean
detail: Ukrainian forces destroyed the Vsesvit-Oil fuel depot near Dnipropetrovsk on March 26, with video documentation showing massive explosions and fire. The strike was part of Ukraine's sustained campaign targeting fuel and energy infrastructure supplying Russian forces. Separately, French and British authorities seized a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in the Mediterranean Sea on or around March 20, extending Western interdiction of Russian oil exports beyond Baltic port strikes. UK government reaffirmed its commitment to policing sanctioned vessels; Ukrainian drone strikes and Western tanker seizures are having significant compounding impacts on Russian oil export revenues.

media.caption: Ukraine destroys Vsesvit-Oil depot as part of sustained campaign against Russian energy infrastructure
